items.headline: AI and Tech Enthusiasm Drive Markets Higher, But Inflation and Hawkish Fed Pose Risks
items.summary: Stocks concluded the week higher, predominantly fueled by continued enthusiasm for AI and technology shares. However, underlying market fragilities persist, with rising inflation and increasingly hawkish signals from the Federal Reserve hinting at potential future volatility. The upcoming earnings season, led by major U.S. banks, is poised to provide critical insights into the broader economic landscape. Not financial advice.

items.headline: SK hynix Inc. Jumps on Market Debut
items.summary: SK hynix Inc. surged 13.0% in its U.S. market debut after opening at $171, with its CEO's comments on a prolonged memory chip supply shortage reinforcing strong demand expectations.

items.headline: MV Oil Trust Plunges on Final Distribution
items.summary: MV Oil Trust experienced a significant decline of 52.60% following its announcement regarding a final trust distribution.

items.headline: Alarum Technologies Ltd. Drops Amid Inquiry
items.summary: Alarum Technologies Ltd. fell 51.50% as the company addressed an inquiry concerning residential proxy networks.

items.headline: Technology and AI Drive Market Gains; Semiconductors Lead
items.summary: Technology and AI-linked companies continue to be the primary drivers of market support, with strong performances noted in the semiconductor and semiconductor equipment sectors due to AI enthusiasm and demand for high-bandwidth memory. Healthcare and Utilities are also among top performing sectors for July.

items.headline: Real Estate and Smaller Caps Lag Amid Rate Concerns
items.summary: Real estate equities, including various REIT sectors, lagged this week, pressured by a rebound in benchmark rates. Mid-Cap and Small-Cap segments also underperformed. Airlines and retailers face new pressures despite recent declines in oil prices.

items.headline: Fed Adopts More Hawkish Stance Amid High Inflation
items.summary: The Federal Reserve is signaling a more hawkish approach, with the FOMC reiterating its commitment to price stability. This comes as May's CPI inflation hit a three-year high of 4.2%. Market expectations for a September 2026 rate cut have diminished to 3.4%.

items.headline: Major Banks and Tech Giants Set to Report Earnings
items.summary: The earnings season is set to commence with major U.S. banks, including JPMorgan Chase ($JPM), Bank of America ($BAC), Citigroup ($C), Wells Fargo ($WFC), and Goldman Sachs ($GS), scheduled to report early next week. Other key reports are anticipated from healthcare companies, semiconductor equipment makers like ASML Holding N.V. ($ASML), airlines such as Delta Air Lines Inc. ($DAL), and streaming giant Netflix, Inc. ($NFLX). Delta Air Lines notably reported being on track to meet its 2026 profit targets.

items.headline: AI Optimism Persists Amid Geopolitical and Inflationary Concerns
items.summary: AI optimism continues to drive market momentum, particularly evident in technology and semiconductor stocks. The 10-year U.S. government bond yield stands at 4.56%, while oil prices are around $71.62. Geopolitical tensions, specifically the ongoing Iran War and its potential impact on oil supply, along with elevated inflation, remain significant macro concerns for the markets.
